summary: A solid sound‑board capture (generally clear with a few volume quirks) that lets a blistering China Cat > Rider and strong Althea/Franklin’s performances carry the show.
review: The SBD of this unknown‑date concert is mostly clean and well balanced, though a handful of listeners note occasional splices or an overly loud piano. Musically the night shines – reviewers repeatedly call the China Cat > Rider “the best” or “scorching,” and they also praise Althea, Lazy Lightnin', C.C. Rider and Franklin’s Tower. The setlist gets mixed feedback (some find the second‑set slower), but overall the performance is regarded as one of the stronger shows of the 1980s.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars and highlight standout performances; recording quality is generally good with only isolated issues, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced SBD for most of the show. A few reviewers mention volume spikes, an occasional splice and Brent’s piano being overly forward in places.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Most fans love the high‑energy second set, especially the China Cat > Rider sequence; a minority feel the later selections (Peggy‑O, Far From Me) slow the momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers rave about his soaring solos on China Cat > Rider, Franklin’s and He's Gone.
band_member_comments.Bob: One reviewer notes a nice vocal interplay between Bob and Jerry in the China Cat > Rider verses.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) is praised for sparkly playing but also criticized for being too loud and drowning out other instruments at times.
